goal: Add a deterministic prompt/context drift validator that compares prompt context, goal-board route, Project Brain, active artifacts, runtime-card evidence, and receipt head-SHA references before any of those surfaces can be treated as current claim support.

forbiddenFiles: GitHub, CodeRabbit, Linear, CircleCI, release, merge, or destructive cleanup mutations
public command authority changes unless the command is a narrow validator wrapper over this module
delivery-truth pass-claim promotion before prompt/context drift has current receipts and explicit claim-support eligibility
raw prompt text, raw transcript text, raw command output, full tool payloads, screenshots, image bytes, secrets, tokens, credentials, or unredacted environment values in report payloads

designConstraints: Prompt/context drift validation must be a deep module under src/lib/prompt-context-drift with a narrow script wrapper; it must not be bolted into a broad command file.
The validator must consume pointer-only references and receipt metadata, not full prompt text, transcripts, tool payloads, raw command output, screenshots, or secrets.
PromptContextReceipt/v1 remains orientation or audit_trail only; this slice may reject or block stale claim-support attempts but must not make prompt context itself a delivery-truth pass source.

The report must compare source refs, source hashes, active route refs, Project Brain refs, runtime-card or handoff refs, receipt ids, observed head SHA, current head SHA, TTL or generatedAt freshness where present, and missing or stale source state.
The validator must classify each surface separately so active route, Project Brain, runtime card, receipt head SHA, prompt context, and external head truth cannot be blended into one ready verdict.
Orientation-only stale context may guide next-step routing when explicitly classified, but it must be ineligible for closeout, merge readiness, Judge/PM readiness, or delivery-truth pass claims.
Repo-relative refs must be traversal-free, absolute-path-free, home-path-free, URL-free unless explicitly classified as external metadata, and bounded to the repository root when validated locally.

Every source ref used for claim support must carry a sha256 hash and must either match the current local file digest or be explicitly classified as external/unverifiable and ineligible for claim support.
Head-SHA checks must fail closed when observedHeadSha and currentHeadSha disagree for claim-support evidence.

Active route checks must fail closed when .harness/active-artifacts.md is missing, lacks Current Active Route, has no repo-relative refs, points at missing route refs, or marks the current route as not current.
Project Brain checks must fail closed for claim support when required .harness/memory or .harness/knowledge refs are missing, unhashed, stale, or unverified.
Runtime-card evidence checks must fail closed for claim support when runtime-card evidence is missing, stale, unhashed, head-mismatched, or only advisory.
The validator should reuse the existing prompt-context receipt and context-health vocabulary where that reduces invention, but the final report contract must be deterministic and fixture-backed.
Generated reports must use closed status vocabularies and stable blocker classes so agents can automate routing from JSON output.

The first agent-readable consumer boundary is the existing agent-readiness contextHealth JSON projection; this slice may project prompt_context_drift status there, but it must not make harness next, delivery-truth, PR closeout, or runtime-card pass claims depend on the report until a later explicit wiring slice.

Claim-support coverage requires at least one repo-contained hash-verified ref for every required local surface: prompt_context, active_artifacts, active_route, project_brain_memory, project_brain_knowledge, runtime_card_or_handoff, and receipt_head_sha. External or unverifiable refs may be recorded, but they must never satisfy required-local-surface coverage.
Local digest verification must canonicalize real paths and prove the target remains under the repository root before hashing; symlink escapes and alias paths outside the repository must fail closed.
Architecture and governance docs must explain where the prompt-context-drift module lives, what it validates, and why it blocks claim support without becoming a prompt composer or scheduler.
acceptanceCriteria: PromptContextDriftReport has schemaVersion prompt-context-drift-report/v1 and records generatedAt, producer, repoRootRef, currentHeadSha, evidenceUse, overallStatus, surfaces, blockers, and nextAction.
The report contract pins closed enum values for overallStatus, per-surface status, evidenceUse, freshness, blockerClass, and nextActionClass; tests reject unknown enum values at each schema boundary.
Validation accepts a current fixture where prompt context, active route, Project Brain refs, runtime-card or handoff refs, receipt refs, source hashes, and head SHA are all current and content-bound.
Validation rejects or blocks claim-support use when prompt context is stale, missing, unknown, expired, or lacks required source hashes.

Validation rejects or blocks claim-support use when .harness/active-artifacts.md is stale, missing the Current Active Route section, contains no repo-relative route refs, points at missing route refs, or marks the route as not current.
Validation rejects or blocks claim-support use when Project Brain memory or knowledge refs are missing, stale, unhashed, externally-only, or fail local digest verification.
Validation rejects or blocks claim-support use when runtime-card or handoff refs are missing, stale, advisory-only, externally-only, head-mismatched, unhashed, or fail local digest verification.
Validation rejects or blocks claim-support use when a required local surface is satisfied only by external, unverifiable, or metadata-only refs.
Validation rejects or blocks claim-support use when observedHeadSha and currentHeadSha differ.
Validation rejects or blocks claim-support use when any claim-support source ref is missing a sha256 hash, has an unsupported hash algorithm, or fails local digest verification.
Validation rejects repo-relative refs whose canonicalized realpath escapes the repository root, including deterministic symlink escape or alias fixtures.
Validation rejects raw prompt, transcript, command output, full tool payload, screenshot/image bytes, secret-like keys, secret-like scalar values, unknown top-level fields, and unknown nested fields.
Validation classifies stale orientation-only evidence as orientation_blocked or audit_only rather than pass.
Standalone script validation can validate the example fixture and produce machine-readable JSON on failure.

Tests cover current positive, stale prompt context, stale active route, stale or missing Project Brain refs, stale/advisory/head-mismatched runtime-card refs, external-only required surfaces, mismatched head SHA, missing source hash, digest mismatch, symlink escape, raw-content rejection, secret rejection, unknown enum rejection, and orientation-only stale cases.

The existing agent-readiness --json contextHealth projection can surface prompt_context_drift as advisory machine-readable status with report evidence, status, stale reasons, and suggested validation command when a report exists or when required report evidence is missing.
The module is discoverable from ARCHITECTURE.md and the agent-governance docs without requiring implementers to search broad command files.

Slice review artifacts include improve-codebase-architecture, simplify, unslopify, he-code-review, testing, adversarial-reviewer, agent-native-reviewer, and best-practices-researcher results or explicit blocked/n.a. evidence before done is claimed.
validationPlan: pnpm exec biome check .harness/intent/codex-runtime-evidence-verifier-cockpit-pu-037-spg-011-prompt-context-drift-validator-intent.json src/lib/prompt-context-drift contracts/prompt-context-drift-report.schema.json contracts/examples/prompt-context-drift-report.example.json contracts/fixtures/prompt-context-drift scripts/validate-prompt-context-drift.cjs src/dev/validate-prompt-context-drift-script.test.ts ARCHITECTURE.md AGENTS.md docs/agents/00-architecture-bootstrap.md docs/agents/07b-agent-governance.md

git diff --check -- .harness/intent/codex-runtime-evidence-verifier-cockpit-pu-037-spg-011-prompt-context-drift-validator-intent.json src/lib/prompt-context-drift src/lib/agent-readiness/context-health.ts src/lib/agent-readiness/types.ts src/commands/agent-readiness.test.ts contracts/prompt-context-drift-report.schema.json contracts/examples/prompt-context-drift-report.example.json contracts/fixtures/prompt-context-drift scripts/validate-prompt-context-drift.cjs src/dev/validate-prompt-context-drift-script.test.ts ARCHITECTURE.md AGENTS.md docs/agents/00-architecture-bootstrap.md docs/agents/07b-agent-governance.md docs/goals/codex-runtime-evidence-verifier-cockpit/state.yaml docs/goals/codex-runtime-evidence-verifier-cockpit/receipts.jsonl artifacts/reviews/pu-037-spg-011-prompt-context-drift-validator-*.md
rollbackPlan: Remove the prompt-context-drift report schema, example, fixtures, validator module, script wrapper, and tests.
Remove prompt-context-drift references from architecture and governance docs.

Preserve PromptContextReceipt/v1, context-health, runtime-card, delivery-truth, review-state, external-state, and PR closeout behavior unchanged unless an explicit later slice wires them through this validator.
currentStateNotes: Current local branch is codex/jsc-363-prompt-context-drift-validator at head 9047a6ffc3c6a6b7d5be7b9f58557e132b116698 before this intent artifact.

Pre-implementation intent review found missing Project Brain/runtime-card negative fixtures, required-local-surface coverage ambiguity, symlink containment ambiguity, and a missing agent-readable consumer boundary; this intent has been amended before implementation to make those checks acceptance criteria.

PR #313 is delegated to a closed green-sweep subagent artifact and remains separate from this local implementation slice; current external truth still has pending CircleCI lanes and mergeStateStatus UNSTABLE.
PR #310 linked-issue ambiguity was repaired by making the PR body explicitly preparatory for JSC-363 rather than claiming JSC-363 acceptance closure.
Untracked files already exist outside this slice and must not be staged, deleted, or used as proof for SPG-011.
~/.codex/.env is not read or sourced by this local contract slice unless live CircleCI API triage becomes necessary.
stopConditions: Stop if prompt/context drift validation requires embedding raw prompts, transcripts, command outputs, tool payloads, screenshots, secrets, tokens, or bulky telemetry.

Stop if stale prompt context, stale active route, stale Project Brain, stale runtime-card evidence, or head-mismatched receipts would be treated as merge_ready, review_addressed, Linear-aligned, goal-ready, Judge/PM-ready, or delivery-truth pass proof.
Stop if local semantic validation would need to read outside the repository root, follow unsafe paths, or rely on external mutable state as deterministic proof.
Stop if the implementation would replace existing PromptContextReceipt/v1 or context-health behavior instead of adding an additive validator/report contract.
